_about: Synthetic baseline captured against a disposable pgserve instance with pg_stat_statements pre-loaded. The live pgserve at port 19642 has shared_preload_libraries empty and could not be safely restarted while Group 1 work and other genie agents are using it (see REPORT.md §3 'Resolution path' for rationale). Workload mirrors the SQL shapes observed in profile-sample-30s.json (audit_events insert + indexed read; agents/executors small-table polls; teams idx scan; agents UPDATE). Replay script: /tmp/loadgen.ts (preserved for reproducibility, see REPORT.md §3.2).

top5_by_total_exec_time.query: SELECT id, event_type, created_at FROM audit_events WHERE entity_type = $2 AND entity_id = $1 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $3
SELECT id, state, team FROM agents WHERE state = $1
INSERT INTO audit_events (entity_type, entity_id, event_type, actor, details) VALUES ($5, $1, $2, $3, $4)
SELECT id, agent_id, state FROM executors WHERE state = $1
UPDATE agents SET state = $1 WHERE id = $2

p50_p99_methodology.p50_estimate: mean_exec_time (Gaussian assumption — accurate when stddev/mean is small, which holds for SELECT/UPDATE/INSERT in this dataset; max ≈ 7× mean for the worst case rank-1 query, 12× for rank-3, indicating moderate right-tail skew not captured by Gaussian P50).
p50_p99_methodology.p99_estimate: mean_exec_time + 2.33 * stddev_exec_time (Gaussian inverse). For heavy-tailed (right-skewed) latencies this UNDERESTIMATES the true P99 — actual P99 sits between this estimate and max_exec_time. A future bench harness (Group 5) using PERCENTILE_CONT over per-query timing spans (the same hook_perf_baseline view shape Group 4 will deliver) will produce true P50/P99.
p50_p99_methodology.caveat: PG's pg_stat_statements only exposes calls, total/min/max/mean/stddev — it does not store per-call samples, so true percentiles cannot be computed from this view alone. The Gaussian estimates here are the best obtainable from pg_stat_statements; they are sufficient for the 'before' anchor but Group 5's harness MUST use spans for the 'after' comparison.
